summary.narrative: Governor Lamont campaigned on affordability across housing, electricity, healthcare, and education. Key 2018 promises included tax restraint and property tax credits. Notable accomplishments include largest state income tax cut, minimum wage increase to $15, recreational marijuana legalization, and COVID-19 emergency management. Current focus emphasizes job creation, cost reduction, and economic growth with projected budget surplus.
summary.grade_rationale: Lamont delivered on key economic promises (income tax cut, minimum wage increase, marijuana legalization) but property tax credit expansion incomplete. Strong performance on pandemic response and budget management. Ongoing affordability focus demonstrates commitment but limited evidence of major structural cost reductions yet.

promises.normalized: Reduce electricity costs for households and businesses
Implement largest income tax cut in Connecticut history
Increase minimum wage to $15 per hour
Restore and expand property tax credit to $300 for all property owners

data_gaps: Specific measurable outcomes for affordability initiatives
Detailed progress metrics on healthcare cost reduction goals
Property tax credit implementation timeline and actual expansion amount
Election margin and vote totals from 2022 gubernatorial election
Office contact phone number and detailed address
Housing cost reduction initiatives and outcomes
Job creation and training program enrollment numbers
Recreational marijuana implementation status and revenue
Higher education affordability program details and participation
